entries.content: The Sun is a G-type main-sequence star at the center of our solar system. It generates energy through nuclear fusion, converting hydrogen into helium. With a diameter of 1.39 million kilometers (109 times Earth), it contains 99.86% of the Solar System's mass.

metadata.location: Center of the solar system, 150 million km from Earth
metadata.water: No liquid water - too hot. Water molecules break apart
metadata.size: 1.39 million km diameter, 333,000 times Earth's mass

entries.content: Mars is the fourth planet from the Sun, known as the Red Planet due to iron oxide on its surface. It's a terrestrial planet with a thin atmosphere, polar ice caps, and the largest volcano in the solar system (Olympus Mons).

metadata.composition: Iron oxide surface, basaltic rock, CO2 atmosphere
metadata.water: Water ice at poles, subsurface liquid water lake discovered
metadata.location: Fourth planet from Sun, 228 million km average distance

entries.content: The Moon is Earth's only natural satellite, formed 4.5 billion years ago likely from debris after a Mars-sized object collided with Earth. It influences Earth's tides and helps stabilize our planet's axial tilt.

metadata.composition: Anorthosite crust, basaltic maria, small iron core
metadata.water: Water ice in permanently shadowed polar craters
metadata.location: Orbits Earth at 384,400 km average distance
